116.reason: [This shifts vertex pointers but does not update vertex numbers (g[i]->no), causing mismatch between index and stored vertex number]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This shifts vertex structures without updating their `no` field. After shifting, `g[i]->no` still holds the original number, so vertex indices become inconsistent. Also, `g[node]` (which is not a valid vertex in the graph) is copied into the graph.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Shifting array pointers does not update the 'no' field inside the vertex structures. g[i]->no will retain old labels, causing incorrect output after deletion.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Shifting the vertex array changes indices and labels, violating the requirement to preserve original vertex numbers and may cause out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This shifts vertex pointers but does not update vertex numbers (g[i]->no), causing mismatch between index and stored vertex number]

142.reason: [Vertex positions are decremented, but this assumes vertices are renumbered after deletion, which conflicts with traversal starting from original vertex 0 that may have been shifted]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Decrementing edge positions assumes renumbering, but the vertex structures in g[] were shifted without updating their `no` fields, so the indices no longer match the stored vertex numbers.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Renumbering vertices alters their labels, which contradicts the problem statement.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Vertex positions are decremented, but this assumes vertices are renumbered after deletion, which conflicts with traversal starting from original vertex 0 that may have been shifted]
